title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 6 - Small box expansions and brief buyer's advice
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lCTSpZlGMb4

Daniel (@dungeondive) offers a comprehensive tour of Runebound's small box expansion ecosystem—those delightful micro-expansions that were once impulse buys at your local game store. From character decks that add class systems and special talents to challenge card packs that expand the encounter variety, these modular additions are masterclasses in sustainable game design. This episode also includes frank collector's and buyer's advice for anyone interested in entering the Runebound second edition hobby.

The standout takeaway: if you're just getting into Runebound today, focus on the base game plus the excellent fan-made expansions from Artscow (cities of adventure, into the wild, and more), then selectively add challenge and item cards as you find them. The big box expansions, while thematically rich, require significant table space and add substantial rules overhead—the fan-made supplements offer more immediate bang for your buck and let you keep everything in the base box.

----

Are you the type of collector who prefers building vertically with one game system, or do you jump between different titles?
